message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): non-empty User-Agent header contract (tick 332)

Added `distributed_http_client_sends_non_empty_user_agent` — pins that
`DistributedHttpClient::get` sends a non-empty `User-Agent:` header.

Contract shape: the header exists and has a non-whitespace value. The
*specific* value is intentionally not asserted because that's a
library-owned detail (asupersync currently defaults to
`asupersync/<version>`) that may evolve. The contract is just "client
identifies itself" — strong enough to catch silent UA stripping,
loose enough not to couple to the library's version format.

Why this matters: server-side observability commonly keys on UA:
- Rate limiters bucket by UA.
- WAFs and bot-detection systems reject requests with missing UA.
- Log aggregation filters by UA to separate internal clients from
  probes.

A regression that stripped UA entirely would silently break
production routing and logging without surfacing any error at the
client side.

Test result: ok. 1 passed; 0 failed
Compile time: 1m 59s cold.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
